title: Computer, Automated Teller, and Office Machine Repairers
description: Repair, maintain, or install computers, word processing systems, automated teller machines, and electronic office machines, such as duplicating and fax machines.

tasks.text: Reassemble machines after making repairs or replacing parts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Converse with customers to determine details of equipment problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Disassemble machines to examine parts, such as wires, gears, or bearings for wear or defects, using hand or power tools and measuring devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Advise customers concerning equipment operation, maintenance, or programming.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Align, adjust, or calibrate equipment according to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair, adjust, or replace electrical or mechanical components or parts, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering or welding equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Travel to customers' stores or offices to service machines or to provide emergency repair service.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain parts inventories and order any additional parts needed for repairs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate machines to test functioning of parts or mechanisms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Reinstall software programs or adjust settings on existing software to fix machine malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean, oil, or adjust mechanical parts to maintain machines' operating efficiency and to prevent breakdowns.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain records of equipment maintenance work or repairs.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Test new systems to ensure that they are in working order.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete repair bills, shop records, time cards, or expense reports.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Install and configure new equipment, including operating software or peripheral equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze equipment performance records to assess equipment functioning.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Read specifications, such as blueprints, charts, or schematics, to determine machine settings or adjustments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Update existing equipment, performing tasks such as installing updated circuit boards or additional memory.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test components or circuits of faulty equipment to locate defects, using oscilloscopes, signal generators, ammeters, voltmeters, or special diagnostic software programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble machines according to specifications, using hand or power tools and measuring devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay cable and hook up electrical connections between machines, power sources, and phone lines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter information into computers to copy programs from one electronic component to another or to draw, modify, or store schematics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fill machines with toners, inks, or other duplicating fluids.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Train new repairers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calibrate testing instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
